assessments.status_rationale: Today’s headlines contain no Federal Reserve/Federal Register action or Fed press release about changing FedNow access rules to allow indirect participation via approved intermediaries.
No news items provided document an FOMC rate cut or even the current target range details; the deadline is 2026-12-31, so outcome cannot be determined yet.

Several stories discuss potential/rumored fees or tolls in the Strait of Hormuz (e.g., Trump warning Iran about charging fees), but none clearly indicate Iran has publicly announced an official policy with a schedule of charges or a collection mechanism.
No item provided indicates the U.S. has imposed an additional 50% tariff on a specific country as retaliation for supplying weapons to Iran.
There is coverage of Florida’s AG investigating OpenAI, but no indication that OpenAI has publicly filed an IPO registration statement (S-1/F-1) on SEC EDGAR.

The market-related bullets discuss an Iran cease-fire rally and earnings season, but do not provide evidence that the S&P 500 has already set a new all-time high close relative to the prior record as of 2026-04-09.
No provided news indicates any U.S. state has enacted a right-to-repair law explicitly covering agricultural equipment with mandated access to parts/tools/documentation.

No provided news indicates any major agricultural or construction equipment OEM (other than John Deere) has announced a binding right-to-repair agreement/settlement with a U.S. state attorney general or multistate coalition.
None of today’s items report the U.S. 10-year Treasury constant maturity rate (DGS10) printing at or below 3.50% on a trading day; the deadline remains 2026-12-31.
